description: Mixa AI — Developer Browser + Knowledge Engine + Infrastructure Control Plane

userStories.title: Initialize monorepo with Turborepo + pnpm
userStories.description: Set up the root monorepo with Turborepo, pnpm workspaces, TypeScript, ESLint, Prettier. Create package.json for root and all workspace packages (apps/desktop, apps/web, packages/ui, packages/types, packages/ai-pipeline, packages/terminal-renderer, packages/content-processor, packages/db). Configure turbo.json. Create top-level Makefile for orchestrating Go + JS builds.
acceptanceCriteria: Root package.json uses pnpm workspaces pointing to apps/* and packages/*
pnpm-workspace.yaml includes apps/*, packages/*, docs/*
turbo.json has build, dev, test, lint, typecheck pipelines
tsconfig.base.json exists with strict TypeScript (strict: true, no any)
Each package has its own package.json and tsconfig.json extending base
.eslintrc or eslint.config exists at root with TypeScript rules
.prettierrc exists at root
Makefile has targets: all, build-engine, build-js, dev, clean
`pnpm turbo build` runs without errors (empty builds OK)
`pnpm turbo lint` runs without errors
.gitignore includes node_modules, dist, .turbo, .next, *.env, engine/bin/

userStories.title: Set up packages/types with all shared TypeScript types
userStories.description: Create the shared types package with TypeScript interfaces for all domains: knowledge items, chunks, tags, projects, conversations, messages, tab system, engine modules, settings, chat, and UI protocol. These types are imported by all other packages.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/types/src/knowledge.ts has types: Item, Chunk, Tag, Highlight, ItemType, SourceType
packages/types/src/projects.ts has types: Project, ItemProject
packages/types/src/chat.ts has types: Conversation, Message, Citation, ChatScope
packages/types/src/tabs.ts has types: Tab, TabType, Space, TabState
packages/types/src/engine.ts has types: EngineModule, EngineStatus, EngineCommand, EngineEvent
packages/types/src/protocol.ts has types: UIView, UIComponent, UIAction (Fenix UI protocol)
packages/types/src/settings.ts has types: UserSettings, LLMProvider, LLMConfig, ThemeConfig
packages/types/src/index.ts re-exports everything
Package builds successfully and all types are exported
No `any` types anywhere — all fields are explicitly typed

userStories.title: Set up packages/db with Drizzle schema and Docker dev environment
userStories.description: Create the database package with Drizzle ORM schema matching the PRD database design (Section 8). Set up Docker Compose for local PostgreSQL + pgvector + Redis. Create initial migration. Seed script with sample data.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/db/src/schema/ contains all tables from PRD Section 8
Tables: users, items, chunks, tags, item_tags, projects, item_projects, highlights, conversations, messages
pgvector extension enabled for embedding column (vector(1536))
All indexes defined per PRD (fulltext, vector cosine, btree on foreign keys)
Drizzle config points to DATABASE_URL environment variable
docker/docker-compose.dev.yml starts PostgreSQL 16 with pgvector + Redis 7
Migration runs successfully against Docker PostgreSQL
Seed script creates sample user, sample items, sample tags
packages/db/src/client.ts exports configured Drizzle client
Package builds and exports schema + client

userStories.title: Initialize Go engine skeleton with gRPC server
userStories.description: Create the Go engine in engine/ with module structure, gRPC server, protobuf definitions for the Electron ↔ Go communication protocol, health check endpoint, and basic module registry. The engine should start, listen on a Unix socket or TCP port, and respond to health checks.
acceptanceCriteria: engine/go.mod initialized with module path
engine/cmd/fenix/main.go is the binary entry point
engine/internal/grpc/server.go implements gRPC server
engine/pkg/proto/ contains .proto files for: HealthCheck, ModuleList, UIStream
Proto defines UIView message matching the Fenix UI protocol from PRD Section 7.4
Generated Go code from protobuf compiles
Engine starts and responds to gRPC health check
engine/internal/bus/ has a basic event bus (pub/sub pattern)
engine/internal/storage/ has encrypted SQLite wrapper (AES-256-GCM)
Makefile target `build-engine` cross-compiles for darwin-arm64, darwin-amd64, linux-amd64
`go test ./...` passes
`go vet ./...` passes

userStories.title: Set up Electron app with basic window management
userStories.description: Initialize the Electron app in apps/desktop. Set up main process, renderer process, preload scripts with contextBridge. Create a basic BrowserWindow that loads a React app. Configure electron-builder for development. Set up Vite for the renderer process.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/desktop/src/main/index.ts creates BrowserWindow with security best practices
nodeIntegration: false, contextIsolation: true in webPreferences
apps/desktop/src/preload/index.ts exposes safe IPC via contextBridge
apps/desktop/src/renderer/ is a Vite + React app
Renderer loads and displays a basic React component
Electron app starts in dev mode with hot reload (electron-vite or similar)
electron-builder.yml configured for macOS DMG (dev only, packaging is later)
Window has proper title, minimum size constraints, and icon placeholder
Dev tools accessible in development mode only
`pnpm turbo build` includes desktop app build

userStories.title: Implement tab system with Chromium webviews
userStories.description: Build the core tab management system. Each tab has a type (web, terminal, knowledge, chat, dashboard, settings). Web tabs use Electron's webContents/BrowserView for Chromium rendering. Other tab types render React components. Tab bar with add/close/reorder. Tab state management with Zustand.
acceptanceCriteria: TabBar component renders horizontal tab strip with favicon, title, close button
Clicking + button creates new web tab with blank page
Web tabs render actual web pages via Electron webContents
Tab close removes tab and activates adjacent tab
Tab reordering via drag-and-drop
Zustand store manages tab state: id, type, title, url, isActive, isLoading
Support for all TabType values from packages/types (web, terminal, knowledge, chat, dashboard, settings)
Non-web tabs render a React component placeholder (actual content comes later)
Keyboard shortcuts: Cmd+T (new tab), Cmd+W (close tab), Cmd+1-9 (switch to tab N)
Active tab indicator (underline or highlight)
Tab overflow handling (scroll or dropdown when too many tabs)

userStories.title: Build omnibar (URL + command palette + search)
userStories.description: Create the unified omnibar that serves as URL bar, command palette, and search input. When focused, it shows suggestions: recent URLs, bookmarks, open tabs, commands (prefixed with >), and knowledge search results.
acceptanceCriteria: Omnibar input field at top of browser chrome
Typing a URL and pressing Enter navigates active web tab
Typing a search query and pressing Enter opens default search engine
Prefix with > to enter command mode (shows available commands)
Shows autocomplete suggestions: open tabs, recent history, commands
Cmd+L focuses the omnibar (standard browser shortcut)
Cmd+K opens omnibar in command mode (> prefix)
Escape closes suggestion dropdown and returns focus to tab
Current page URL shown when omnibar is not focused
Loading indicator when web tab is loading
SSL indicator (lock icon) for HTTPS pages

userStories.title: Build sidebar with navigation and tab tree
userStories.description: Create collapsible sidebar showing: tab tree (grouped by spaces), quick-access buttons for special tab types (terminal, knowledge, chat), and navigation controls.
acceptanceCriteria: Sidebar component renders on left side of browser
Tab tree shows all open tabs grouped by type or space
Quick-access buttons: New Terminal, Knowledge Base, Chat, Settings
Sidebar is collapsible (toggle with Cmd+B or button)
Collapsed state shows only icons
Active tab highlighted in sidebar
Click tab in sidebar activates it
Right-click context menu on tabs: Close, Close Others, Move to Space
Sidebar width adjustable by dragging edge
Sidebar state persists across app restarts

userStories.title: Implement browser navigation controls and history
userStories.description: Add standard browser navigation: back, forward, reload, stop, home. Implement browsing history storage and display. Handle new window requests, link clicks, and navigation events from webContents.
acceptanceCriteria: Back/Forward/Reload/Stop buttons in browser chrome
Buttons are enabled/disabled based on navigation state
Keyboard shortcuts: Cmd+[/] (back/forward), Cmd+R (reload), Esc (stop)
Browsing history stored locally (last 10,000 entries)
History accessible via command palette or dedicated view
External link clicks open in current tab (same-origin) or new tab (target=_blank)
Window.open requests create new tabs instead of new windows
Downloads handled by Electron's download manager
Page title and favicon extracted and shown in tab bar
Find-in-page: Cmd+F opens search overlay for current web tab

userStories.title: Connect Electron to Go engine lifecycle
userStories.description: Implement Go engine lifecycle management from Electron main process. Start the Go binary as a child process on app launch, establish gRPC connection, handle engine health checks, restart on crash, and graceful shutdown on app quit.
acceptanceCriteria: Electron main process spawns Go engine binary on app start
Engine binary path resolved from app resources (bundled) or dev path
gRPC client connects to engine after spawn (with retry logic)
Health check polling every 10 seconds
If engine crashes, auto-restart with exponential backoff (max 3 retries)
Graceful shutdown: send shutdown signal to engine before Electron quits
Engine status exposed to renderer via IPC (running, starting, crashed, stopped)
Status indicator in browser chrome shows engine health
Logs from engine process captured and accessible in app
Works in both development mode (engine from source) and production (bundled binary)

userStories.title: Implement content capture from web tabs
userStories.description: Build the content capture system. Users can save the current page (full article extraction via Readability) or a text selection. Captured content is stored in PostgreSQL via the db package. Capture accessible via keyboard shortcut, context menu, and omnibar command.
acceptanceCriteria: Cmd+S saves current web page to knowledge base
Content extracted using Readability algorithm (title, author, content, excerpt)
Text selection → right-click → 'Save to Mixa' captures selected text as highlight-type item
Captured items stored in items table with correct item_type and source_type
Domain and favicon extracted and stored
Word count and estimated reading time calculated
Capture shows brief toast notification confirming save
Duplicate URL detection with option to update existing item
Content sanitized (no scripts, safe HTML only)
Works on standard web pages, fails gracefully on restricted pages (chrome://, about:)

userStories.title: Build content processor package
userStories.description: Implement packages/content-processor with extractors for different content types: articles (Readability), code blocks (syntax detection), and basic metadata. YouTube and PDF extractors are deferred to later phases.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/content-processor/src/extractors/article.ts uses @mozilla/readability
Article extractor returns: title, author, content (HTML), textContent, excerpt, siteName
packages/content-processor/src/extractors/code.ts detects and extracts code blocks with language
packages/content-processor/src/sanitizer.ts strips scripts, iframes, tracking pixels from HTML
packages/content-processor/src/thumbnail.ts generates thumbnail URL from page metadata (og:image)
All extractors have TypeScript interfaces matching packages/types
Unit tests for article extraction (test with sample HTML fixtures)
Unit tests for code block detection
Package builds and exports all extractors

userStories.title: Build LLM provider abstraction (BYOK)
userStories.description: Implement packages/ai-pipeline with a provider abstraction layer. Support OpenAI, Anthropic, Ollama (local), and Gemini as LLM backends. Users configure their own API keys. Provider interface supports: chat completion, embedding generation, and streaming.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/ai-pipeline/src/providers/ has adapters: openai.ts, anthropic.ts, ollama.ts, gemini.ts
Common interface: LLMProvider with methods: chat(), embed(), stream()
OpenAI adapter supports gpt-4o-mini, gpt-4o, text-embedding-3-small
Anthropic adapter supports claude-3.5-sonnet, claude-3-haiku
Ollama adapter connects to local Ollama instance (configurable endpoint)
Gemini adapter supports gemini-2.0-flash
Provider router selects provider based on user settings
API keys read from user settings (never hardcoded, never logged)
Graceful error handling: provider unavailable, rate limited, invalid key
Unit tests with mocked HTTP responses for each provider

userStories.title: Implement text chunking and embedding generation
userStories.description: Build the text chunking strategy and embedding generation pipeline. Captured content is split into semantically meaningful chunks, each chunk gets an embedding vector, and both are stored in the chunks table with pgvector.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/ai-pipeline/src/chunker.ts splits text into chunks (512 tokens target, 50 token overlap)
Chunking respects paragraph and sentence boundaries (not mid-sentence cuts)
packages/ai-pipeline/src/embedder.ts generates embeddings via configured LLM provider
Embeddings stored in chunks table with pgvector column
BullMQ job queue for async embedding generation (capture doesn't block on AI)
Worker processes embedding jobs from Redis queue
Job retries on failure (max 3 retries with exponential backoff)
Token counting for chunks (tiktoken or equivalent)
Unit tests for chunking logic (various text lengths, edge cases)
Integration test: capture item → chunk → embed → verify stored in DB

userStories.title: Implement auto-summarization and auto-tagging
userStories.description: When content is captured, generate an AI summary and auto-assign topic tags. Summary stored on the item. Tags created or matched to existing tags and linked via item_tags with confidence scores.
acceptanceCriteria: After embedding job completes, a summarize+tag job runs automatically
Summary is 2-3 sentences, stored in items.summary
Tags are 3-7 topic labels per item, created in tags table if new
Tag assignment includes confidence score (0-1) in item_tags.score
LLM prompt is well-structured: takes article content, outputs JSON with summary + tags
Tags are normalized (lowercase, deduplicated, merged with existing similar tags)
Works with any configured LLM provider (OpenAI, Anthropic, Ollama, Gemini)
Graceful degradation: if AI fails, item is saved without summary/tags (not lost)
Unit tests for tag normalization and deduplication
Cost-efficient: uses cheapest model available (gpt-4o-mini or haiku)

userStories.title: Implement semantic search and full-text search
userStories.description: Build the search system combining vector similarity search (pgvector) with full-text search (PostgreSQL tsvector). Users can search their knowledge base and get ranked results combining both approaches.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/ai-pipeline/src/retriever.ts implements hybrid search
Vector search: embed query → cosine similarity against chunks.embedding → return top K
Full-text search: PostgreSQL FTS with ts_rank against items content
Hybrid scoring: combine vector similarity and FTS rank with configurable weights
Search returns items with relevance score, highlighted matching snippets
Minimum score threshold to filter low-quality matches
Search scoping: filter by tags, projects, date range, item_type
Search performance: <500ms for 10,000 items
Unit tests for scoring logic
Integration test: insert items with embeddings → search → verify ranked results

userStories.title: Build RAG chat pipeline
userStories.description: Implement the full RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) pipeline for the chat interface. User asks a question → retrieve relevant chunks → construct prompt with context → generate response with citations.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/ai-pipeline/src/pipeline.ts orchestrates: query → retrieve → rerank → generate
Retrieval uses hybrid search (MIXA-016) to find top 10-20 relevant chunks
Context window management: fit as many chunks as possible within model's context limit
System prompt instructs LLM to cite sources and stay grounded in provided context
Response includes citation markers referencing specific items and chunks
Citation format: [1], [2], etc. mapped to source items with URLs
Streaming response support (tokens streamed as generated)
Chat history maintained in conversations + messages tables
Scoped chat: user can limit retrieval to specific projects or tags
Unit tests for context window packing and citation extraction

userStories.title: Build chat tab UI
userStories.description: Create the Chat tab React component with message input, streaming response display, source citations, conversation history sidebar, and scope selector.
acceptanceCriteria: ChatTab.tsx renders a full-height chat interface
Message input at bottom with send button and keyboard shortcut (Enter to send, Shift+Enter for newline)
Messages display with proper markdown rendering (code blocks, lists, links)
AI responses stream token-by-token with typing indicator
Citations rendered as clickable chips/links below the response
Clicking a citation opens the source item (in a new knowledge tab or web tab)
Conversation history sidebar: list of past conversations, click to resume
New conversation button clears chat and starts fresh
Scope selector: dropdown to limit chat to specific projects or tags
Loading state while waiting for AI response
Error state with retry button if AI call fails

userStories.title: Build knowledge browse/search tab UI
userStories.description: Create the Knowledge tab that shows all saved items with search, filters, and browse functionality. Grid/list view toggle, tag filtering, project filtering, and item detail view.
acceptanceCriteria: KnowledgeTab.tsx renders saved items in grid (cards) or list layout
Toggle between grid and list view
Search bar at top triggers hybrid search (MIXA-016)
Filter sidebar: by tags, projects, item_type, date range, favorites
Item cards show: thumbnail, title, domain, summary excerpt, tags, capture date
Click item opens detail view: full content, highlights, metadata, related items
Bulk actions: select multiple → delete, move to project, add tag
Infinite scroll or pagination for large collections
Empty state with helpful onboarding message for new users
Sort options: newest, oldest, most relevant (when searching)

userStories.title: Define Fenix UI protocol (protobuf + TypeScript types)
userStories.description: Formalize the JSON-based UI protocol between the Go engine and the terminal renderer. Define protobuf messages for all UI component types (header, table, card, chart, form, list, metric-row, status-bar). Generate both Go and TypeScript types from the proto definitions.
acceptanceCriteria: engine/pkg/proto/ui.proto defines all UIComponent types from PRD Section 7.4
Component types: Header, Table, Card, Chart, Form, List, MetricRow, StatusBar, TextBlock, ActionBar
UIView message wraps a module name + array of UIComponents + array of UIActions
UIAction has id, label, shortcut, enabled fields
UIEvent message for user interactions: click, input, shortcut, scroll
Proto compiles to Go code (engine/pkg/proto/)
Proto compiles to TypeScript types (packages/types/src/protocol.ts or generated)
Types match between Go and TypeScript (validated by comparing field names/types)
Documentation in proto files explains each message and field
Unit test in Go verifying serialization/deserialization of sample UIView

userStories.title: Build terminal renderer React components
userStories.description: Create packages/terminal-renderer with React components that render each Fenix UI protocol component type as beautiful, interactive HTML/CSS. This is the key innovation: terminals rendered as web UI.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/terminal-renderer/src/components/Table.tsx renders sortable, filterable data tables
packages/terminal-renderer/src/components/Card.tsx renders info cards with actions
packages/terminal-renderer/src/components/Chart.tsx renders area/bar/line charts (using recharts or similar)
packages/terminal-renderer/src/components/MetricRow.tsx renders metric cards with trends
packages/terminal-renderer/src/components/List.tsx renders selectable lists with keyboard nav
packages/terminal-renderer/src/components/Form.tsx renders input forms
packages/terminal-renderer/src/components/ActionBar.tsx renders action buttons with shortcuts
packages/terminal-renderer/src/components/StatusBar.tsx renders bottom status bar
packages/terminal-renderer/src/protocol.ts parses UIView → renders correct components
All components respect active theme (dark/light)
All components are keyboard-accessible
Storybook or similar for visual component development and testing

userStories.title: Build terminal tab with gRPC streaming
userStories.description: Create TerminalTab.tsx that connects to the Go engine via gRPC streaming, receives UIView updates, renders them using terminal-renderer components, and sends user interactions back as UIEvents.
acceptanceCriteria: TerminalTab.tsx establishes gRPC stream to engine for the active module
Receives UIView messages and renders via terminal-renderer protocol parser
User clicks on interactive elements generate UIEvent messages sent back to engine
Keyboard shortcuts captured and forwarded as UIEvents
Module selector: dropdown or sidebar to switch between engine modules
Loading state while engine processes commands
Error state if engine connection is lost (with reconnect option)
Multiple terminal tabs can be open simultaneously (each with its own gRPC stream)
Terminal tab header shows active module name and status
Smooth transitions when engine sends view updates

userStories.title: Implement GUARD module in Go engine
userStories.description: Build the GUARD (secrets & environment management) module in the Go engine. Manage environment variables, integrate with Doppler/vault, switch between environments. Expose via Fenix UI protocol.
acceptanceCriteria: engine/internal/guard/ implements the GUARD module
List all environment variables for current project (masked values by default)
Reveal individual secret values on demand (with confirmation)
Switch between environments (dev/staging/prod)
Import from .env files
Export sanitized .env.example (values replaced with placeholders)
All secrets stored encrypted in SQLite (AES-256-GCM)
UIView output renders as a table with columns: Key, Value (masked), Environment, Source
Actions: Add, Edit, Delete, Reveal, Copy, Switch Env
Go tests for encryption, import/export, CRUD operations

userStories.title: Implement FORGE module in Go engine
userStories.description: Build the FORGE (Git & GitHub) module. View repos, branches, commits, PRs, issues. File browser for repos. Expose via Fenix UI protocol.
acceptanceCriteria: engine/internal/forge/ implements the FORGE module
List Git repositories (local directory scanning)
Show current branch, recent commits, changed files for a repo
List GitHub PRs with status, reviewers, labels
List GitHub issues with status, assignees, labels
File browser: navigate repo tree, view file contents
Actions: checkout branch, pull, push, create PR (opens GitHub URL in web tab)
UIView renders repo info as cards + file tree as a navigable list
Uses local git commands and GitHub API (via gh CLI or direct API)
Go tests for git operations (using test repo fixture)

userStories.title: Implement KEYS module in Go engine
userStories.description: Build the KEYS (shortcuts & command palette) module. Define default keyboard shortcuts, allow customization, expose command palette data to the Electron omnibar.
acceptanceCriteria: engine/internal/keys/ implements the KEYS module
Default shortcut map for all modules and common actions
User-customizable shortcuts stored in config (fenix.yaml or SQLite)
Shortcut conflicts detected and reported
Command palette endpoint: returns all available commands with descriptions
Commands filterable by module, category, or search string
Import/export shortcut configuration
Integration with Electron omnibar: commands from engine appear in Cmd+K palette
Go tests for shortcut conflict detection and command filtering

userStories.title: Implement xterm.js fallback for raw shell
userStories.description: Add xterm.js-based raw shell terminal as a fallback for when users need actual bash/zsh access (not the Fenix UI protocol). This runs alongside the web-rendered terminal.
acceptanceCriteria: TerminalTab can switch between 'fenix' mode (UI protocol) and 'shell' mode (xterm.js)
Shell mode uses xterm.js with proper terminal emulation
Shell connects to user's default shell (bash/zsh) via node-pty
Proper ANSI color rendering, cursor movement, scrollback buffer
Copy/paste support (Cmd+C/Cmd+V)
Font size adjustable
Omnibar command: 'Open Shell' creates terminal tab in shell mode
Keyboard shortcut: Cmd+` opens new shell tab

userStories.title: Implement COST module in Go engine
userStories.description: Build the COST (cloud cost tracking) module. Track costs per provider, per project. Budget alerts. Cost history. Expose via Fenix UI protocol with rich charts.
acceptanceCriteria: engine/internal/cost/ implements the COST module
Provider adapters for: DigitalOcean, AWS (basic), manual entry
Cost polling on configurable schedule (hourly/daily)
Cost data stored in encrypted SQLite with time-series structure
Budget limits per provider/project with threshold alerts (80%, 90%, 100%)
UIView renders: MetricRow (total, projected, budget), Chart (cost over time), Table (per-service breakdown)
Actions: Refresh, Export CSV, Set Budget, View History
Go tests for cost calculations, adapter interfaces, budget threshold logic

userStories.title: Implement PULSE module in Go engine
userStories.description: Build the PULSE (health & uptime) module. Ping endpoints, track SSL certificate expiry, monitor uptime. Expose via Fenix UI protocol.
acceptanceCriteria: engine/internal/pulse/ implements the PULSE module
HTTP(S) health checker: ping configurable endpoints on schedule
SSL certificate expiry tracking with alerts at 30/14/7 days
Uptime calculation: percentage over last 24h, 7d, 30d
Response time tracking with p50/p95/p99 percentiles
Incident log: records when endpoints go down/up with timestamps
UIView renders: uptime grid (green/red squares), response time chart, SSL expiry table, incident timeline
Actions: Add Endpoint, Remove, Test Now, View Incidents
Go tests for health checking, uptime calculation, SSL parsing

userStories.title: Build cost dashboard tab
userStories.description: Create a Dashboard tab specifically for costs that renders rich visualizations from the COST module data. This is the web-rendered alternative to the terminal COST view, with full interactive charts.
acceptanceCriteria: DashboardTab variant for costs: apps/desktop/src/renderer/tabs/dashboards/CostDashboard.tsx
Total monthly spend metric card with trend indicator
Per-provider cost breakdown (pie/donut chart)
Daily cost accumulation (area chart)
Budget utilization bars with color coding (green < 80%, yellow 80-90%, red > 90%)
Month-over-month comparison
Table with per-service breakdown (sortable, filterable)
Budget limit editing (inline slider + input)
Data fetched from Go engine via gRPC
Responsive layout, loading states, empty states

userStories.title: Build health dashboard tab
userStories.description: Create a Dashboard tab for health/uptime monitoring with data from the PULSE module.
acceptanceCriteria: DashboardTab variant for health: HealthDashboard.tsx
Uptime grid: visual grid of green/yellow/red squares per endpoint per day
Response time chart: line chart with p50/p95/p99
SSL certificate expiry table with countdown badges
Incident timeline: chronological list of up/down events
Endpoint management: add/edit/remove endpoints
Quick actions: Test Now, Acknowledge Incident
Data fetched from Go engine via gRPC
Responsive layout, loading states

userStories.title: Build knowledge stats dashboard
userStories.description: Create a dashboard view showing knowledge base statistics: items captured over time, most-used tags, reading habits, search patterns.
acceptanceCriteria: KnowledgeDashboard.tsx shows knowledge base overview
Total items count with trend
Items captured per day/week (bar chart)
Top tags cloud or bar chart
Most saved domains (top 10)
Reading time estimates (total saved reading time)
Recent captures feed (last 10 items)
Quick actions: Open Knowledge Tab, New Capture, Start Chat
Data fetched from PostgreSQL via tRPC

userStories.title: Build settings tab
userStories.description: Create the Settings tab with configuration for LLM providers, Fenix engine modules, appearance, keyboard shortcuts, data management, and account.
acceptanceCriteria: SettingsTab.tsx with tabbed/sectioned layout
AI Providers section: configure API keys for OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Ollama endpoint
API keys stored securely in OS keychain (Electron safeStorage)
Active provider selector with model picker
Engine section: list of Fenix modules with enable/disable toggles
Appearance section: theme selector (dark/light/system), font size, sidebar position
Shortcuts section: list all shortcuts, click to rebind, reset to defaults
Data section: storage usage display, export all data button, clear cache button
All settings persisted (Electron store or SQLite)
Changes take effect immediately without restart

userStories.title: Implement theming system
userStories.description: Build a theming system that applies across the entire app: browser chrome, terminal renderer, knowledge views, dashboards, and chat. Support dark/light/system themes with customizable accent colors.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/ui/src/themes/ defines theme tokens (colors, spacing, typography, radii)
Built-in themes: dark (default), light, system (follows OS)
Theme applied globally via CSS variables or Tailwind config
Terminal renderer components respect active theme
Dashboard charts use theme-aware color palettes
Chat messages styled according to theme
Web tabs are not affected by theme (they render their own content)
Theme switching is instant (no flash/reload)
Accent color customizable (10 preset options + hex input)
Theme preference persisted in settings

userStories.title: Set up GitHub Actions CI pipeline
userStories.description: Create CI workflow that runs on PRs: lint, typecheck, test for all TypeScript packages, and go test/vet for the engine.
acceptanceCriteria: .github/workflows/ci.yml triggers on pull_request to main
Runs: pnpm install → pnpm turbo lint → pnpm turbo typecheck → pnpm turbo test
Runs: cd engine && go vet ./... && go test ./...
Uses pnpm cache for faster installs
Uses Go cache for faster builds
Fails PR if any check fails
Runs on ubuntu-latest
PostgreSQL + Redis services for integration tests
Job matrix: Node 20, Go 1.22

userStories.title: Set up Electron packaging and build workflow
userStories.description: Configure electron-builder for packaging the app as DMG (macOS), AppImage (Linux), and NSIS installer (Windows). Create GitHub Actions workflow for building releases.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/desktop/electron-builder.yml configured for mac (dmg+zip), linux (AppImage), win (nsis)
Go engine binary bundled as extraResource in the Electron app
Makefile target: `make package` builds Go engine → builds Electron app → packages
.github/workflows/release.yml triggers on git tag (v*)
Release workflow: build for all platforms → upload artifacts to GitHub Release
Code signing placeholder for macOS (notarization config, actual certs later)
App icon set for all platforms
Package size optimized (no unnecessary devDependencies bundled)
Smoke test: packaged app launches and shows browser window

userStories.title: Implement auto-update mechanism
userStories.description: Set up electron-updater so the app checks for updates on launch and notifies users when a new version is available. Updates downloaded in background and applied on restart.
acceptanceCriteria: electron-updater configured with GitHub Releases as update source
Check for updates on app launch (after 5-second delay)
Check for updates every 4 hours while running
Notification shown when update is available (non-blocking)
User can choose: Update Now (restart) or Later
Download progress shown in notification
Update applied on next app restart
Version info visible in Settings tab
Fallback: manual download link if auto-update fails

userStories.title: Set up tRPC layer for renderer ↔ main process communication
userStories.description: Implement tRPC router in the Electron main process with procedures for all knowledge base operations. Renderer uses tRPC client via IPC (not HTTP) for type-safe communication.
acceptanceCriteria: tRPC router in main process with routers: items, projects, tags, search, chat, settings, engine
IPC-based tRPC transport (not HTTP — uses Electron IPC for same-process communication)
items router: create, list, get, update, delete, search
projects router: create, list, get, update, delete
tags router: list, merge, rename
search router: hybrid search with filters
chat router: createConversation, sendMessage (streaming), listConversations
settings router: get, update
engine router: status, listModules, sendCommand
All procedures use Zod input validation
Full TypeScript type inference from router to client

userStories.title: Build augmented browsing indicator
userStories.description: While browsing any web page, Mixa checks if the user's knowledge base contains related content and shows a subtle indicator. Clicking the indicator opens a sidebar panel with related saves.
acceptanceCriteria: Content script (injected into web pages) extracts page title + meta description
Extracted text sent to search system to find related items
If related items found: subtle indicator appears (badge on sidebar or floating icon)
Clicking indicator opens panel showing related items from knowledge base
Panel shows: item title, summary, relevance score, capture date
Clicking an item in the panel opens it in a knowledge tab
Indicator doesn't appear on restricted pages or when knowledge base is empty
Check happens 2 seconds after page load (debounced, not on every navigation)
Toggle augmented browsing on/off in settings

userStories.title: Create onboarding flow for new users
userStories.description: When the app is launched for the first time, guide users through setup: configure LLM provider, explore tab types, capture first item, try a chat query.
acceptanceCriteria: First launch detection (no settings file exists)
Welcome screen with Mixa AI branding and value proposition
Step 1: Configure AI provider (pick provider, enter API key, test connection)
Step 2: Quick tour of tab types (web, terminal, knowledge, chat)
Step 3: Prompted to capture a page ('try saving this page')
Step 4: Prompted to ask a question in chat
Skip option available at every step
Onboarding state tracked (don't show again after completion or skip)
Tutorial tips in empty states (empty knowledge base, empty chat, etc.)

userStories.title: Create comprehensive README and documentation
userStories.description: Write root README, per-package READMEs, development setup guide, and architecture documentation.
acceptanceCriteria: Root README.md: project overview, architecture diagram (ASCII), quick start, contributing guide
apps/desktop/README.md: Electron app setup, dev workflow, packaging
engine/README.md: Go engine setup, module development, gRPC API reference
packages/ai-pipeline/README.md: provider setup, RAG pipeline docs
packages/terminal-renderer/README.md: component catalog, theming
packages/content-processor/README.md: extractor docs
packages/db/README.md: schema reference, migration guide
docs/ARCHITECTURE.md: detailed architecture with diagrams
docs/DEVELOPMENT.md: full setup from zero to running (prerequisites, Docker, env)
docs/FENIX-UI-PROTOCOL.md: protocol specification with examples
docs/BYOK.md: how to configure each LLM provider

userStories.title: Implement App tabs with session partitions
userStories.description: Build the App tab type that runs web apps in isolated Electron session partitions. Each App tab has its own cookies, localStorage, and auth — enabling multiple accounts of the same service (e.g., two WhatsApp Web instances).
acceptanceCriteria: App tab type uses Electron session.fromPartition() with persist: prefix
Each App tab instance gets a unique partition name
Cookies and localStorage persist across app restarts per partition
Can open two WhatsApp Web tabs logged into different accounts simultaneously
App tab templates for popular apps: WhatsApp Web, Slack, Discord, Gmail, Google Meet, Notion
Templates include: URL, icon, name, recommended partition strategy
Sidebar shows App tabs with custom icons (not just favicons)
New App tab via omnibar command: 'new app [name]'
App tabs are visually distinct from regular web tabs in tab bar

userStories.title: Google Meet integration in media bar
userStories.description: Detect active Google Meet tabs, show them in a persistent media bar, and provide quick controls (mute/unmute, camera toggle, leave meeting) from the media bar without switching tabs.
acceptanceCriteria: Media bar component appears when a Google Meet tab is active
Media bar shows: meeting name, duration, participant count
Quick controls: mute/unmute mic, toggle camera, leave meeting
Controls interact with Meet page via injected content script
Media bar also shows tabs playing audio (any tab with audible media)
Media bar position configurable (top/bottom)
Media bar collapsible
Works for both regular web tabs and App tabs running Meet

userStories.title: Build Canvas tab as visual workspace with embedded tabs
userStories.description: Integrate Excalidraw (MIT-licensed) as a native Canvas tab type, extended with the ability to embed browser tabs INTO the canvas. Users can drag tabs from the tab bar into the canvas, then draw notes, arrows, and diagrams around them — creating a spatial visual workspace.
acceptanceCriteria: Canvas tab renders embedded Excalidraw React component with custom extensions
Drag a tab from tab bar into canvas → embedded as live webview or static screenshot
Embedded tabs can be resized and repositioned within canvas
Two embed modes: Live (interactive webview) and Snapshot (screenshot with link)
Spatial zoom: zoom out for full canvas view, zoom into embedded tab to interact
Add sticky notes, text blocks, freeform drawing around embedded elements
Draw arrows and connectors between any elements (tabs, notes, shapes)
New Canvas from omnibar: 'new canvas' or 'new drawing'
Canvas auto-saves to local filesystem (~/.mixa/data/canvases/)
Export canvas as PNG, SVG, or Excalidraw JSON
Canvas list viewable in sidebar under Canvases section
Dark theme support matching Mixa's active theme

userStories.title: Implement Clerk licensing and workspace auth
userStories.description: Integrate Clerk for lightweight licensing (Pro token verification) and workspace identity. Clerk only answers: does this user have a valid Pro license? Free tier works without any auth.
acceptanceCriteria: Clerk SDK integrated in Electron main process
On app launch: check Clerk for Pro license status (single API call)
If Clerk unreachable or no account: app works in free tier mode (no error)
Pro features gated by license status (not by Clerk being available)
Sign up / Sign in flow accessible from Settings tab (not forced)
Clerk organization used for workspace sharing (MIXA-045)
License status shown in Settings tab: Free / Pro / Team
License check cached locally: re-verify every 24 hours, not on every launch
No user data sent to Clerk beyond email + license status

userStories.title: Workspace sharing via Clerk invite links
userStories.description: Allow users to share their workspace with others via simple Clerk organization invite links. No complex invitation system — one link to join.
acceptanceCriteria: Generate shareable invite link from Settings > Workspace
Link uses Clerk organization invite flow
Recipient clicks link → signs in/up via Clerk → joins workspace
Workspace roles: Owner, Member (simple, no granular permissions)
Shared workspace members can see shared knowledge projects
Shared workspace members can view shared canvases
Owner can revoke access (remove member from Clerk org)
Workspace member list visible in Settings > Workspace

userStories.title: Replace PostgreSQL with PGlite (embedded WASM)
userStories.description: Replace the external PostgreSQL dependency with PGlite — PostgreSQL compiled to WASM that runs embedded in the Electron main process. This eliminates the Docker/PostgreSQL installation requirement entirely.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/db uses @electric-sql/pglite instead of postgres driver
PGlite data directory: ~/.mixa/data/pglite/
pgvector extension loaded in PGlite for embedding storage
All Drizzle schema and migrations work with PGlite
No Docker required for development or production
Database survives app restarts (persistent filesystem storage)
Migration from Docker PostgreSQL to PGlite documented (for existing dev data)
Performance acceptable: <100ms for typical queries on 10k items
docker-compose.dev.yml removed or made optional (for those who prefer external PG)

userStories.title: Implement tab management features (TabFlow)
userStories.description: Port key tab management features from TabFlow: tab groups, sessions, forever history, tab reminders, auto-categorization, and tab suspension.
acceptanceCriteria: Tab groups: create named, colored groups; collapse/expand groups in tab bar
Sessions: save current tab set as named session, restore later from omnibar
Forever history: all tab opens/closes logged in PGlite, searchable via omnibar
Tab reminders: right-click tab → 'Remind me' → pick time → native notification
Tab suspension: tabs inactive for >N minutes get suspended (configurable, default 30min)
Suspended tabs show placeholder, reload on focus
Quick Access pins: pinned sites always shown at top of sidebar
All tab management data stored in PGlite
Import sessions from Chrome (optional)

userStories.title: Implement Simple Mode / Power Mode UI toggle
userStories.description: Two UI modes: Simple Mode reduces visible options by ~60%, shows text labels, progressive disclosure. Power Mode shows everything (icon-only, all views, all actions). Based on TabFlow's Simple Mode design.
acceptanceCriteria: Mode toggle in Settings > Appearance: Simple / Power
Simple Mode: 5 primary views in sidebar (instead of 10+), text labels on all actions
Simple Mode: tab actions always visible (not hover-only), larger click targets (44px+)
Simple Mode: progressive disclosure — advanced features behind 'More' dropdowns
Power Mode: icon-only controls, all views visible, compact layout
Mode selection on first launch (onboarding)
Mode affects: sidebar, tab bar, tab actions, omnibar suggestions, dashboard controls
Smooth transition between modes (no reload needed)
Mode preference persisted in settings

userStories.title: Implement backup to user's own storage provider
userStories.description: Users connect their own S3-compatible storage, Dropbox, Google Drive, or local directory for encrypted backups. Mixa never stores user data.
acceptanceCriteria: Settings > Data > Backup Provider: dropdown with S3, Dropbox, Google Drive, Local Directory
S3-compatible: bucket name, endpoint, access key, secret key (works with AWS S3, Backblaze B2, MinIO)
Local directory: file picker to choose backup location
Dropbox and Google Drive: OAuth flow to authorize Mixa (read/write to a dedicated folder)
Backup format: encrypted ZIP (AES-256-GCM), user sets encryption passphrase
Backup includes: PGlite database dump, canvas files, settings, engine config
Manual backup button: 'Backup Now'
Scheduled backups: daily or weekly (configurable)
Restore: select backup file → enter passphrase → restore all data
Backup history visible in settings (date, size, provider)

userStories.title: Implement Doppler integration for token management
userStories.description: Users who use Doppler can connect their Doppler project to Mixa. API keys and tokens are pulled from Doppler instead of manual entry. Alternative: users manage tokens via local settings.
acceptanceCriteria: Settings > Tokens > Source: 'Manual' (default) or 'Doppler'
Doppler mode: user enters Doppler service token or uses doppler CLI auth
Doppler mode: pulls all configured tokens on app launch (LLM keys, provider keys, etc.)
Token mapping: Doppler secret names mapped to Mixa token slots (configurable)
Manual mode: tokens entered directly in Settings (existing flow)
Both modes store tokens in OS keychain
Token list view: shows all configured tokens, source (manual/Doppler), last verified
Token refresh: re-pull from Doppler on demand or on schedule

userStories.title: Build marketing site with real UI simulation demos
userStories.description: Build the Next.js marketing site (apps/web) that showcases Mixa using actual product UI components, not screenshots. Use the fluffy-umbrella/Fintivio pattern: BrowserMockup wrappers, declarative demo-acts animation system, auto-measurement, and interactive feature demos. Visitors should feel like they're using the product.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/web is a Next.js app with landing page, pricing, and docs
BrowserMockup component wraps product UI in fake browser chrome (traffic lights, address bar)
Demo-acts system: declarative animation configs that drive interactive demos
AnimatedCursor component: visual cursor that moves, clicks, drags on demo elements
Auto-measurement: data-w attributes on DOM elements, runtime position calculation
InterruptibleDemoLoop: plays acts in sequence, pause/resume, jump-to-act on feature click
Hero section: full BrowserMockup showing Mixa with tabs, sidebar, omnibar
Feature sections: Browsing, Terminal, Knowledge/Chat, Canvas, App Tabs — each with interactive demo
Feature cards in sidebar: clicking a feature jumps to its demo animation
All demo UIs use real components from packages/ui (tab bar, sidebar, omnibar, terminal renderer, etc.)
Pricing section with Free/Pro/Team cards
Responsive design, fast loading, SEO optimized
